title: unable to find user root: no matching entries in passwd file
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: # Uncomment only one, leave it on its own line:

# **kind bug**

[//]: # **kind feature**

**Description**
Sometime, we hit the following error while deploying a bunch of containers:
```
2018-11-13 18:16:19 | "2018-11-13 18:13:39,018 ERROR: 4514 -- error mounting image volumes: unable to find user root: no matching entries in passwd file",
```


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Appears at random.

**Describe the results you received:**
It's randomly failing

**Describe the results you expected:**
It should run as expected, using the "--user <value>"  we set with `podman exec`

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
So it's random, and happens a couple of time on our CI.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.11.1
Go Version:    go1.10.2
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.5-dev
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.11.1-2.gita4adfe5.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 02be8b05693994d520f0802560f1195296aadbae-dirty'

    distribution: '"centos"'
    version: "7"
  MemFree: 976379904
  MemTotal: 8365137920
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-55.dev.git2abd837.el7.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 5581828096
  SwapTotal: 8588881920
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: undercloud.localdomain
  kernel: 3.10.0-862.14.4.el7.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 2h 24m 55.42s (Approximately 0.08 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - 192.168.24.1:8787
  - 192.168.24.3:8787
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 88
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 35
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
    ": internal libpod error\" ", 
```
I take it you do have a root user in /etc/passwd?  Or are you missing /etc/passwd?
@cjeanner What is the actual command you are running?
Alright, I think this is us, not runc. Which makes a lot more sense, since we're scanning the container's rootfs for an /etc/passwd, which might not exist...

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/container_internal.go#L1266-L1269 is the cause of our failure.

Interesting note: this is not the first time we call this function for the container - we do another lookup almost immediately after starting spec generation, before this is called.

@cjeanner Is the error message consistent? Or does it vary between runs? We call this function more than once per container, so I'm curious as to whether it's failing in different places each time, or consistently when we try and set up image volumes

@rhatdan so the user does exist in the container. Else it would be consistent, on a specific set of container. But this isn't the case.

@mheon nope, not consistent. It hits "sometimes", and not always for the same container - so it's really, really hard to reproduce (I've spend my day trying really hard... no chance)
So this is runc blowing up?
It could be the runc passwd parse code? We're using it as part of libpod's lookup functions...
Any news on that one?
Going to try and find some time to look at this in the afternoon. Can you confirm it's still occurring?

Hello @mheon 
Yep, can confirm, it still appears randomly.
Thank you for your time!
Any news on this as its recurrence chance appears to be quite high, with several occurenced each day on openstack builds http://status.openstack.org/elastic-recheck/index.html#1803544
The number of hits isn't small but not critical yet. We're working on it now though.
#1978 may offer a fix - it removes the offending piece of code. This could only move the race elsewhere, but it may well be enough to stop it.
I'm going to reopen this until @cjeanner and @EmilienM can confirm the issue is fixed on their end
@mheon fixed by removing the :z on /run/netns mounts.

Huh. This is not an error I would have expected to be related to that.

@rhatdan Should we add a warning against :z/:Z on some system mountpoints?

I might have been confused with another bug. From what I can tell we
haven't seen that bug around anyway.

---
Emilien Macchi

On Thu, Dec 27, 2018, 5:44 PM Matthew Heon <notifications@github.com wrote:

> Huh. This is not an error I would have expected to be related to that.
>
> @rhatdan <https://github.com/rhatdan> Should we add a warning against
> :z/:Z on some system mountpoints?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1817#issuecomment-450187846>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ABnCoV1y82sG8-y5ZgSkBfMYJSA7E5BRks5u9PjNgaJpZM4Yfvy_>
> .
>

Note that we still hit that but ([5 hits in 7 days](http://logstash.openstack.org/#dashboard/file/logstash.json?query=build_name%3A%20*tripleo-ci*%20AND%20build_status%3A%20FAILURE%20AND%20message%3A%20%5C%22no%20matching%20entries%20in%20passwd%20file%5C%22), not that bad).
So it seems there is still a race somewhere, but removing one of the cases where we would hit that race has majorly reduced the probability of it happening.
@cjeanner Have you seen this since the fix.  Reopen if you have.

We are also having same issue. The docker image is based on 

FROM jboss/wildfly:9.0.2.Final

Did someone really found a solution?

What Podman version?

On Fri, Mar 22, 2019, 08:43 Naveed Kamran <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> We are also having same issue. The docker image is based on

> Did someone really found a solution?

We are not using podman. We are using only docker.
Here is the partial code of the Dockerfile

```
FROM jboss/wildfly:9.0.2.Final
# FROM jboss/wildfly:latest

ENV JBOSS_HOME /opt/jboss/wildfly
ENV JAVA_CACERTS /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/java/cacerts

# go root to do copy and rights stuff
USER root

# install needed packages

#RUN yum -y update; yum clean all
# RUN yum -y install openssh-clients; yum clean all
# RUN yum -y install epel-release
# RUN yum -y install ansible

# create folders and copy data into
RUN mkdir /start
RUN mkdir /certs

ADD bin/ /start
ADD customization $JBOSS_HOME/customization/
ADD modules/ $JBOSS_HOME/modules
```

It gives this error:


fatal: [my-all-test]: FAILED! => {"changed": true, "cmd": "sudo docker build -t controller:v1.0 /opt/docker/pm-controller-basic-image/", "delta": "0:00:25.920795", "end": "2019-03-22 13:22:13.867015", "msg": "non-zero return code", "rc": 1, "start": "2019-03-22 13:21:47.946220", "stderr": "linux spec user: unable to find user root: no matching entries in passwd file", "stderr_lines": ["linux spec user: unable to find user root: no matching entries in passwd file"], "stdout": "Sending build context to Docker daemon

But we are running it on Ubuntu 16. Do you know if it also works on ubuntu or anyway around for ubuntu?

```
ppet/container-puppet.sh:/var/lib/container-puppet/container-puppet.sh:ro', 'brew-pulp-docker01.web.prod.ext.phx2.redhat.com:8888/rhosp15/openstack-iscsid:20190715.1'
```
